scene.description: A photorealistic golden-hour hero exterior of the completed Anna's Farm Site Two common-space entry — the narrower 1500 sqft installation. Three-quarter angle along the long axis, camera low, looking past the foreground flagstone grouping into the main 3-boulder cluster with the stucco wall catching warm back-light. The dry-riverbed accent line of rust rip-rap traces along the long edge like a ribbon. Solar path lights along the straight run just begin to glow warm amber.
scene.location: Lafayette, Colorado, Boulder County (40.0004 N, -105.110 W)

materials.type: rust-ochre rip-rap in long-edge accent line
buff Lyons sandstone, 3 tons, low stepping clusters
matte-black 2700K solar path lights, just activating

lighting.source: low golden-hour sun from camera back-right
lighting.direction: raking along the long axis, long shadows stretching camera-left
lighting.practical_lights: warm amber path lights beginning to glow, subtle

camera.framing: low 3/4 angle along the long-axis, leading line of path lights into hero cluster, stucco wall upper background

composition.foreground: first flagstone grouping catching low sun

include: warm raking highlights on flagstone cleft
subtle light-pool activation on path lights
